producer_note: Cru Classe de Graves estate in commune of Leognan, Pessac-Leognan, owned by the de Bethmann family. 60 ha on gravelly clay-limestone soil; produces red and dry white grand vins.

Founded 1997 by Far Niente partners; based in Oakville on 42-acre Sullenger property. Single-vineyard Cabernets sourced from Oakville, Rutherford, Howell Mountain, Stags Leap, St Helena and other Napa AVAs.
Champagne house founded 1868 by Leonie Duchene and Victor Canard; based in Premier Cru village of Ludes (Montagne de Reims). Owns 30 ha and works with 200 grower families.
Napa Valley winery owned by Frank Sinatra's children Nancy, Tina and Frank Jr. Estate vineyards near northern Napa Valley; flagship Cabernet 'Come Fly With Me'.
Cru Classe de Graves estate in Leognan; owned by Bernard family since 1983, run by Olivier Bernard. 35 ha producing red (55% CS, 40% Merlot) and renowned dry whites (70% Sauv Blanc, 30% Semillon).
Family estate dating to 16th century. 320 ha across four estates between Leverano and Salice Salentino in Puglia. Best known for Negroamaro-based blends.
Quatrieme Cru Classe in Saint-Estephe; founded 1650, rebuilt by Tesseron family from 1960; owned by Lorenzetti family since 2021. 45 ha planted to a Cabernet Sauvignon-led blend.
Second Growth (1855) in Saint-Julien, owned by the Cuvelier family since 1920. 80 ha planted to 63% Cabernet Sauvignon, 25% Merlot, 7% Petit Verdot, 5% Cabernet Franc.
Cinquieme Cru Classe in Pauillac, owned by Casteja/Borie-Manoux family. 57 ha planted to 70% Cabernet Sauvignon, 25% Merlot. Produces ~22,000 cases of grand vin annually.
Reference family winery in Friuli's Collio appellation, known for Pinot Grigio, Friulano and Sauvignon Blanc. Cru and Gran Cru lines include Tre Bicchieri-winning Braide Alte.
Deuxieme Cru Classe (1855) in Margaux, owned by the Lurton family with Henri Lurton managing since 1992. 75 ha on gravel mounds; 55% Cabernet Sauvignon, 40% Merlot.
Founded 1999 by the Duncan family of Silver Oak. Sources Pinot Noir from Russian River Valley, Anderson Valley and Sonoma Coast appellations; also Sauvignon Blanc and Chardonnay.
Estate in the Chianti Classico hills of Radda, owned by the Zonin family for 40+ years. 900 ha estate with 125 ha vines at 350-650m; 90% Sangiovese plus Cabernet Sauvignon and Canaiolo.
Tuscan producer based in Castelnuovo Berardenga (southern Chianti Classico) with 140 ha of vines, plus Campogiovanni estate in Brunello di Montalcino. Created the original Super Tuscan, Vigorello.
Napa Valley boutique producer based in the city of Napa; Cabernet Sauvignon-led portfolio plus Pinot Noir and Sauvignon Blanc. Tasting by appointment.

Grower-producer in Grand Cru village of Ambonnay (Montagne de Reims); 9 generations since 1757. 6.12 ha of 62% Pinot Noir, 38% Chardonnay; biodynamic-certified since 2008. Eric Rodez formerly oenologist at Krug.
Family estate in Meursault founded 1919; run by Catherine Buisson and Patrick Essa, with son Louis Essa from 2020. 6.3 ha across Cote de Beaune; organically tended; produces 26 cuvees.
Small-production Pinot Noir project from Michael Browne (co-founder of Kosta Browne) in Sonoma County's Russian River Valley. Two estate vineyards; flagship Cirq RRV Pinot Noir.
Saint-Emilion garagiste estate founded 1997 by stonemason Michel Gracia. 2.84 ha planted to 80% Merlot, 15% Cabernet Franc, 5% Cabernet Sauvignon; ~8,000 bottles annually.
Founded 1983 by Ric Forman (former Sterling winemaker) on a ridge at the base of Howell Mountain overlooking St. Helena, Napa Valley. ~2,000 cases of Cabernet Sauvignon and Chardonnay annually.
Estate in Valgatara, Marano di Valpolicella, in Valpolicella Classica zone. Run by twins Elena and Federico Coati since 1998; produces Amarone, Ripasso, Valpolicella Classico and Recioto.
Founded 1912 by Marcel Ott; owned by Louis Roederer since 2018. Three estates: Clos Mireille and Chateau de Selle in Cotes de Provence AOC, Chateau Romassan in Bandol AOC. ~300 ha all organic.
Founded 1898 by Pietro Sartori; family-owned for four generations. Produces Valpolicella, Soave, Bardolino and Bardolino Chiaretto Classico DOC across multiple Veronese appellations.
Wine-consulting and family estate brand based at Chateau Le Bon Pasteur in Pomerol, the Rolland family home. Michel Rolland (1947-2026) founded Rolland & Associes laboratory in Libourne in 1973.

Negociant founded 1725 by Thomas Barton; partnership formalised 1802 with Daniel Guestier. Bordeaux flagship Chateau Magnol (Haut-Medoc); Passeport range covers Bordeaux, Loire, Beaujolais, Burgundy, Rhone, Provence.
Scamperle family estate in Fumane (Valpolicella Classica) for 150+ years. 20 ha across Fumane, Sant'Ambrogio and San Floriano. Famous for Amarone and Recioto; flagship Pergole Vece.
Estate of 272 ha (35 ha vine) in Val d'Arno di Sopra DOC, on south-eastern border of Chianti Classico. Flagship Galatrona Merlot from 1994; certified organic since 2016.

Domaine Jean Chartron founded 1859 in Puligny-Montrachet, run by 5th generation Jean-Michel and Anne-Laure. 14.5 ha including monopoles Clos de la Pucelle and Clos du Cailleret, plus Chevalier-Montrachet and Batard-Montrachet Grand Crus.
Founded 1980s by Pasqua family; named for 17th-century agronomist Count Giuseppe Beretta. 89 ha across Valpolicella and Soave (volcanic hills). Produces ~200,000 bottles/year.
Pomerol estate, farmed by Etablissements Jean-Pierre Moueix since 1962 and fully owned since 2024. 7.9 ha of 90% Merlot, 10% Cabernet Franc on clay-gravel soils near the church.
Oldest winery in Bolgheri, built 1796 within the Bolgheri walls; owned by Della Gherardesca family. 50 ha producing Bolgheri DOC and Bolgheri Superiore from CS, Merlot, CF, Syrah and Petit Verdot.
Cinquieme Cru Classe (1855) in Labarde, Margaux. Owned by the Roulleau family. 45 ha in Margaux planted to 69% Cabernet Sauvignon, 29% Merlot, 2% Petit Verdot.
Pomerol plateau estate adjacent to Petrus, owned by Bailliencourt dit Courcol family since 1918. 26.5 ha planted to 90% Merlot, 6% Cabernet Sauvignon, 4% Cabernet Franc on gravel-clay soils.
Created 1977 in Gevrey-Chambertin by Philippe Charlopin. 25 ha across Cote de Nuits, Cote de Beaune and Chablis, with grand cru holdings in Clos Vougeot, Clos Saint-Denis and Charmes-Chambertin.

Cooperative based in Vinci with 200+ growers and 1,236 acres of vineyards. Produces Chianti DOCG, Chianti Riserva, plus Pinot Grigio (Tre Venezie) and Brunello di Montalcino across multiple Italian zones.
Unclassed Saint-Julien estate built by Henri Martin from 1942 by acquiring parcels from neighbouring classed growths. 50 ha planted to 65% Cabernet Sauvignon, 25% Merlot. Fully organic from 2024.

Founded 1973 by film producer Italo Zingarelli near Castellina in Chianti. Six estates totaling 500 ha (200+ ha vines); Le Macie, Sant'Alfonso, Riserva di Fizzano and Le Tavolelle in Castellina (Chianti Classico).
Estate near Monti in Chianti, south of Gaiole, owned by Marco Ricasoli Firidolfi. 100 ha (20 ha vine) at 340-510m elevation. Certified organic since 2010; renowned for Vin Santo del Chianti Classico.

Francis Ford Coppola Winery in Geyserville (Sonoma County, Alexander Valley); sold to Delicato Family Wines in 2021. Coppola also owned Inglenook in Rutherford, Napa Valley; brand sources widely across California.
Fornaser family estate in Bure Alto, Valpolicella Classica, since the 1920s. 7.5 ha across three vineyards (Monte Faustino, Traversagna, Costalunga). Specialises in Amarone, Recioto and Ripasso.

Founded 1886 in St. Helena, Napa Valley; one of Napa's oldest wineries. Renamed in 1939 by Charles Freeman, Mark Foster and Abbey Ahern. Cabernet Sauvignon focus; participated in 1976 Judgment of Paris.
Founded 1999 by Michael Twelftree and Richard Mintz in Barossa Valley. Sources Shiraz from Barossa, McLaren Vale, Clare Valley, Eden Valley, Adelaide Hills and Heathcote (Victoria).
Family estate in Premeaux-Prissey run by Arnaud Pelletier (5th generation). 15 ha across Hautes-Cotes de Nuits, Cotes de Nuits-Villages and Nuits-Saint-Georges Premier Crus.
Owned by Farnese Vini Group; two wineries in province of Taranto. Sources Primitivo and Negroamaro from multiple growers across the Salento peninsula. 'I Muri' is the flagship label.
Troisieme Cru Classe (1855) in Margaux, founded 1661, owned by Lurton family. ~30 ha planted to 60% Cabernet Sauvignon, 39% Merlot, 1% Cabernet Franc.
Family winery in Neviglie, between Alba and Asti. 30 ha (23 ha vine) producing Moscato d'Asti DOCG, Barbaresco DOCG, Barbera, Dolcetto, Nebbiolo and Alta Langa metodo classico.
Cooperative based in Barolo (formerly Terre da Vino), formed 2016. 180 winegrowers and ~300 ha across Langhe and Monferrato; produces Barolo, Barbaresco, Barbera d'Asti, Roero and Moscato d'Asti.
Quatrieme Cru Classe (1855) in Saint-Julien, founded 1680 by Jean-Baptiste Braneyre. 60 ha planted to 65% Cabernet Sauvignon, 28% Merlot, 4% Petit Verdot, 3% Cabernet Franc.
Family estate in Cervognano, Montepulciano, since 1962; producing Vino Nobile di Montepulciano DOCG since 1968. ~50,000 bottles/year. Awarded Tre Bicchieri 9 of past 10 years.
Founded 2012 in Serralunga d'Alba by Bulgarian winemaker Bisso Atanassov. Vineyards in Monforte d'Alba (Bussia), Novello (Ravera), La Morra, Castiglione Falletto and Barbaresco.
Domaine Louis Michel & Fils founded 1850 in Chablis, run by Guillaume Gicqueau-Michel. 25 ha across Chablis hierarchy including 7 Premier Crus and 3 Grand Crus (Grenouilles, Les Clos, Vaudesir).

Owns 16 chateaux/estates across Languedoc-Roussillon including Chateau l'Hospitalet (La Clape), Clos d'Ora (Minervois La Liviniere), Chateau La Sauvageonne (Terrasses du Larzac). Major reference in biodynamics.
Founded 1959 in Chablis by William Fevre; sold to Henriot 1998 and acquired by Domaines Barons de Rothschild (Lafite) in 2024. 70 ha including 15.9 ha Premier Cru and 15.2 ha Grand Cru.
Saint-Emilion estate at Saint-Christophe-des-Bardes on the limestone plateau, owned by Peter Sisseck (Pingus) and Silvio Denz since 2010. 7 ha farmed organically; ~750 cases/vintage.
Family estate centred on a 1622 Palladian villa in Crocetta del Montello. 10 ha in Valdobbiadene Prosecco DOCG plus 40 ha around Crocetta del Montello (Montello-Colli Asolani DOC).
Lorusso family estate in Locorotondo (Itria Valley), 450m elevation, third generation. 30 ha of native Apulian varieties; bottling facility added in Fasano in 2008.
Family domaine in Chablis dating to 1845, run by 4th-generation Marie-Ange Robin since 2004. 20 ha including some of the oldest Chardonnay vines in Chablis (80-year-old Blanchot).
Estate in Napa Valley's Chiles Valley AVA at 800ft elevation, purchased 2014. 1,000-acre property with 100+ acres of vineyards; 700 acres preserved with Napa Land Trust.
Cinquieme Cru Classe (1855) in Pauillac, adjacent to Mouton Rothschild. Acquired by Baron Philippe de Rothschild in 1933; now owned by Sereys de Rothschild children. 80+ ha on four gravel banks.
Largest Chianti Classico cooperative, founded 1965 by Gualtiero Nunzi with 18 winegrowers. ~120 members and ~300 ha based around Greve, Mercatale, San Casciano, Tavarnelle and Barberino Val d'Elsa.

Carneros AVA estate at Stanly Ranch, Napa, originally part of Merryvale brand. Acquired 50 acres of Carneros vineyard (originally planted 1872) and built green winery 2006. Focus on Chardonnay and Pinot Noir.

Domaine Daniel-Etienne Defaix in Chablis, 14th-generation family estate. 28-30 ha planted exclusively to Chardonnay, mostly Premier Cru sites. Releases Premier Crus after 8-10 years aging on lees in tank.
Vivaldi (Cantina Valpolicella Negrar group) based in Bussolengo, Verona. Produces Valpolicella, Ripasso, Amarone, Bardolino, Custoza and Pinot Grigio across multiple Veronese DOCs.
Estate in Castelnuovo Berardenga (southern Chianti Classico), owned by Bacci family since 1984. 600+ ha of estate with vineyards planted to Sangiovese, Cabernet Sauvignon and Merlot. Fully organic.
Beaune-based negociant founded 1731; acquired by Henriot 1995, now part of Artemis Domaines (Pinault). 130 ha across Burgundy including 12 ha Grand Cru and 74 ha Premier Cru.

Barone Ricasoli, based at Castello di Brolio in Gaiole; in family hands since 1141. Largest winery in Chianti Classico (235 ha vines across Gaiole and Castelnuovo Berardenga). Bettino Ricasoli devised the Chianti formula in 1872.
Founded 2003 by John Duval (former Penfolds Grange winemaker). Sources Shiraz from Barossa Valley and cooler Eden Valley; flagship Entity Shiraz first released 2004.

Dry Creek Vineyard founded 1972 by David Stare in Healdsburg; first new winery in Dry Creek Valley AVA post-Prohibition. Family-owned, led by Kim Stare Wallace. Pioneer of Sauvignon Blanc and 'Old Vine' Zinfandel terms.
Saint-Emilion Grand Cru Classe (since 2012), acquired 2005 by Silvio Denz. 49 ha planted to 85% Merlot, 10% Cabernet Franc, 5% Cabernet Sauvignon. Mario Botta-designed gravity-flow winery (2009).

Brand of Siam Winery, Thailand's largest winery. Sources from vineyards in Hua Hin (Baan Khok Chang valley), Tab Kwang and Chiang Mai. Varieties include Colombard, Chenin Blanc, Sangiovese and Shiraz.
Family-owned Bordeaux negociant founded 1958 by Roger Bouey, with vine-growing since 1821. Owns chateaux across Medoc and Bordeaux plus properties in Bergerac and Cahors; partners with 100+ chateaux.

Joint venture between Chateau Cheval Blanc and Terrazas de los Andes; first vintage 1999. 32 ha of old vines in Las Compuertas (Lujan de Cuyo DOC) plus 15 ha in Altamira (Uco Valley); blend of Malbec, Cabernet Sauvignon, Petit Verdot.
Founded 1997 by brothers Pico and Vitaliano Maccario in Mombaruzzo (Barbera d'Asti DOCG). 70 ha of contiguous vineyards, 60 ha planted to Barbera; flagship Lavignone is from a 54-ha single block.

20-generation family estate in Bue, Sancerre; current domaine founded 1996, led by brothers Florent and Clement. 17 ha (12.5 Sauv Blanc, 4.5 Pinot Noir) on Terres Blanches and Caillottes. Organic certified 2004.
McGuigan Wines founded 1992 (family in Hunter Valley since 1880s); cellar door in Pokolbin, Hunter Valley. Vineyard holdings span Barossa, Adelaide Hills, Eden Valley, Clare Valley, Victoria and NSW.
Casa Silva, 5th-generation family winery founded 1892 in Colchagua Valley. Pioneer of Chilean Carmenere; vineyards across multiple Colchagua sub-zones (Los Lingues, Lolol, Paredones coastal).

Premier Grand Cru Classe B (since 2006) in Saint-Emilion, owned by Corre family (Macquin descendants); managed by Nicolas Thienpont with Stephane Derenoncourt. 15 ha planted to 84% Merlot, 14% Cabernet Franc.

Founded 2001 by Aurelio Montes Sr. (Montes of Chile). Headquartered in Lujan de Cuyo's Vistalba region with vineyards extending from Cafayate to Patagonia. Focus on Malbec and Cabernet Sauvignon; flagship Mai is 100% Malbec.

Tinazzi-owned estate acquired 2014 in Sant'Ambrogio di Valpolicella (Valpolicella Classico), at 650m elevation. 5 ha vines plus 11 ha woodland; produces Valpolicella Classico Superiore, Ripasso and Amarone Riserva.

Historic Alba-based winery founded 1904; taken over by Antinori family in 1989/1994. 65 ha across Langhe and Monferrato producing Barolo, Barbaresco, Barbera d'Asti and Roero from multiple Piedmont DOCG zones.

Domaine Clusel-Roch in Verenay (northernmost Cote-Rotie village), run by Guillaume Clusel. 14 ha across Cote-Rotie, Condrieu and Coteaux du Lyonnais; certified organic since 2005. Flagship Les Grandes-Places dates to 1935.
Chablis estate run by Etienne Boileau, William Nahan and Christian Simon since 1987. 36 ha across Petit Chablis (9 ha), Chablis (17.5 ha) and Chablis Premier Cru (9 ha), exclusively Chardonnay.
Founded 1966 by Wolfgang Blass in Nuriootpa, Barossa Valley; now part of Treasury Wine Estates. Sources widely across South Australia for tiered Red/Yellow/Gold/Black/Platinum labels.

Family winery in San Damiano d'Asti, founded 1968 by Giuseppe Ponte; third generation (Massimo, Ivan, Renato) running today. Produces Barbera d'Asti DOCG, Barbera d'Asti Superiore DOCG, Piemonte DOC and Brachetto.

Perrin family (since 1909) own Chateau de Beaucastel (110 ha in Chateauneuf-du-Pape) plus negociant range covering southern Rhone (Gigondas, Vacqueyras, Cotes du Rhone, Vinsobres). Pioneers of organic and biodynamic farming.
Family-founded 1932 by Isaac Muga and Aurora Cano in the Barrio de la Estacion, Haro. Farms 250 ha in Rioja Alta plus contracts for 150 ha. Operates own cooperage with ~14,000 barrels.

Family-owned winery in Tanunda, Barossa Valley, on the North Para river. Home of The Freedom 1843 vineyard, claimed as the world's oldest surviving Shiraz vineyard. Owned/operated by the Lindner family.

Compania Vinicola del Norte de Espana founded 1879 in Haro by Real de Asua brothers; still family-controlled. Three Rioja wineries: CVNE/Cune (Haro, Rioja Alta), Vina Real (Laguardia, Rioja Alavesa) and Contino (Laserna). ~1,000 ha total.

Wynns Coonawarra Estate founded 1891 by John Riddoch; renamed by David Wynn in 1951. Largest single vineyard holder in Coonawarra on the terra rossa strip; produced Australia's first commercially labelled Cabernet (Black Label, 1954).

Family winery in 9th generation (Dr. Dirk Richter, son Constantin); 300-year history. ~48 acres in the Middle Mosel between Erden and Brauneberg; 95% Riesling. Owns monopoles Muhlheimer Helenenkloster and Veldenzer Elisenberg.
TOR Wines founded 2001 by Tor Kenward (former Beringer executive of 25 years) and wife Susan in Napa Valley. Single-vineyard Cabernet Sauvignon (incl. Beckstoffer To Kalon) and Chardonnay.

Pinot Noir-focused brand created 2008 by Jayson Woodbridge (Hundred Acre, Layer Cake). Sources from Stanly Ranch (Carneros), Huckleberry Snodgrass (Sonoma Coast), plus Russian River Valley, Monterey and Santa Barbara.
Founded 2004 by acclaimed consulting winemaker Celia Welch (F&W Winemaker of the Year 2008). Cabernet Sauvignon sourced from three Napa Valley sub-AVAs: Oakville, Rutherford and Pritchard Hill.

Founded 1995 by Colin Glaetzer (now run by son Ben) in Tanunda, Barossa Valley. All fruit sourced from Ebenezer sub-district of northern Barossa, focus on Shiraz; flagship Amon-Ra is one of Australia's most acclaimed Shiraz.
Vina Aromo (El Aromo) in Talca, Maule Valley; winery founded 1922, acquired by Don Victor in 1936. 332 ha of estate vineyards. Awarded Catad'Or Best Winery of Chile 2021. Exports to 30 countries.

Founded 1973 by Fred and Juelle Fisher; family-owned, second generation managing. Two estates: Calistoga Estate (Napa Valley) and Mountain Estate (Mayacamas Mountains, Sonoma County). Best known for Cabernet Sauvignon.
